summary: Cohort enumerated (121 unique files, two error classes correlated with extension). Hypothesis A (native binding) RULED OUT — pure WASM stack. Hypothesis B (WASM) elevated and split B1/B2. Parser site located at tree-sitter-parser.ts:714 inside try/catch.

title: Hypothesis A (native binding mismatch) RULED OUT — pure WASM stack
recommendation: Pivot Hypothesis A from native-binding bisect to web-tree-sitter × tree-sitter-wasms version bisect. No native tree-sitter Node addon is installed.

title: Two-error-class signal — error_message correlates with file extension
recommendation: Treat Hypothesis B as B1 (bash-grammar dynamic-linker fault: 'resolved is not a function' = 70/70 .sh) and B2 (cross-grammar memory access OOB: 51 files across .sh/.ts/.py/.js). Discriminate separately in iter 2-3.

title: Parse() call site located — fail-soft try/catch wraps WASM parse
recommendation: Skip-list early-return belongs above tree-sitter-parser.ts:714. Skip-list write belongs in catch at lines 741-756. Outer-layer parse and recordParseDiagnostic surfaces also identified.

title: Exact version pins of WASM stack
recommendation: Vendored web-tree-sitter@0.24.7, tree-sitter-wasms@0.1.13 (built 2026-03-31). tree-sitter-typescript pulled transitively only — not declared directly in package.json.

title: Cohort distribution — 121 unique files, dominated by spec-kit scripts
recommendation: Original 1640/9349 figure was per-event during scan, not unique-file. Cohort is 85% .sh, 8% .ts, 5% .py, 2% .js. .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/* is the largest sub-cohort.

title: Qualitative sample (5 files) — three sub-patterns emerge
recommendation: Each error class shows a distinct stressor (bash nested parameter expansion; .ts long absolute-path imports; .py/.js size + nested literals). Single-feature Hypothesis C is unlikely; class-internal stressor is plausible.
